mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 0.5% monthly increase in CPIAUCSL, signal central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising CPIAUCSL, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in FEDFUNDS and decline in CPIAUCSL signals a balanced monetary policy tightening with disinflation, which reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.3% and CPIAUCSL falls > 0.2% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in rolling 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% monthly increase in PAYEMS signals strong labor market demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly increase in INDPRO signals robust industrial production, which strengthens corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances with INDPRO growth, or INDPRO fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% monthly increase in HOUST signals strong housing demand, which boosts construction sector activity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances with HOUST growth, or HOUST fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% quarterly increase in GDPC1 signals sustained economic growth, which raises corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances with GDPC1 growth, or GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and decline in UNRATE signals strong consumer spending and labor market tightness, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 2% and UNRATE falls > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in rolling 5-year regressions.
